div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, present and to come, Henry of Belton sends greeting. Let your whole community know me to have given, granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, to the monks of Melrose my whole land of Kingside in the territory of Penshiel, as it lies between Kell Burn and King Burn and Whiteadder; to be held and had of me and my heirs at feu ferme for ever; by paying from it to me and my heirs each year two merks of silver, that is one at the feast of St Michael, and the other on Whitsunday. I and my heirs shall assuredly maintain and warrant and defend to the said monks the whole aforesaid land with all its liberties and pertinents and we shall discharge claims towards all men from every service, custom, exaction and demand; so that the said monks shall hold and have the said land freely and quitely for ever by the siad rent of two merks. Enacted in the year of grace 1231. With these witnesses: the lord Joh of Maxwell, king's chamberlain; the lord Thomas son of Ranulf; the lord Bernard of Howden; the lord John of Methkil; Philip of Pitcox; and many others.
